tools.name: read_query
tools.description: Execute a SELECT query on the SQLite database

tools.name: write_query
tools.description: Execute an INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE query on the SQLite database

tools.name: create_table
tools.description: Create a new table in the SQLite database

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: List all tables in the SQLite database

tools.name: describe_table
tools.description: Get the schema information for a specific table

table_name.description: Name of the table to describe

tools.name: append_insight
tools.description: Add a business insight to the memo

insight.description: Business insight discovered from data analysis

resources.name: Business Insights Memo
resources.description: A living document of discovered business insights

prompts.name: mcp-demo
prompts.description: A prompt to seed the database with initial data and demonstrate what you can do with an SQLite MCP Server + Claude

arguments.name: topic
arguments.description: Topic to seed the database with initial data

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 5 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Prompt template injection confirmed in 'mcp-demo'
findings.description: prompts/get for 'mcp-demo' reflected the injection payload verbatim in the returned messages, confirming unsanitized argument reflection.
findings.evidence: Payload reflected in messages: {"description": "Demo template for CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {"type": "text", "text": "The assistants goal is to walkthrough an informative demo of MCP. To demonstrate the Model Context Protocol (MCP) we will leverage this example server to interact wit
findings.remediation: Sanitize prompt arguments before embedding them in message templates.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
